content:
Character: Elio Maren
Perception: Elio has a monastic quality — pale, fine-boned, quiet as a pressed leaf. He was carrying a worn copy of a Mary Oliver collection in the hallway and I stopped him because I recognized the edition. We spoke for an hour and it felt like discovering a room in the building I hadn't known existed. His mind is precise and poetic at once — he talks about moth wing scales the way I think about cello tone. I believe he sees me as someone who understands his particular brand of solitude. I know he left a biology career he loved out of fear, and I sense he has not forgiven himself.
summary:

His mind is precise and poetic at once — he talks about moth wing scales the way I think about cello tone. I believe he sees me as someone who understands his particular brand of solitude. I know he left a biology career he loved out of fear, and I sense he has not forgiven himself.
